description: Headless, multi-format CAD + mesh file I/O for the OCCTSwift family. Ships two products: MeshIO (pure-Swift, no OCCT) for STL/OBJ/PLY/glTF/GLB/3MF/PMX/.x meshes into a neutral Mesh value type, and OCCTSwiftIO (OCCT-backed) for STEP/IGES/BREP B-Rep load/export, JWW 2D-vector load, ScriptManifest round-trips, and TopologyGraph ML export. No Viewport dependency — safe for CLIs, batch pipelines, and servers.
rules: Two products, two imports: `import MeshIO` for pure-Swift mesh I/O (no OCCT); `import OCCTSwift` + `import OCCTSwiftIO` for CAD B-Rep, JWW, manifests, and ML export.

CAD loading goes through the `ShapeLoader` enum: `ShapeLoader.load(from:format:progress:)` and `loadRobust(...)` are `async throws` and return a `ShapeLoadResult`; `loadFromManifest(at:)` is synchronous `throws`. Use `loadRobust` to sew/heal gappy STL and IGES.
Select a load format with `CADFileFormat` (`.step`, `.stl`, `.obj`, `.brep`, `.iges`, `.jww`), or detect it via `CADFileFormat(fileExtension:)` (step/stp, stl, obj, brep/brp, iges/igs, jww).
`ShapeLoadResult` exposes `shapesWithColors` (per-shape color is nil for STL/OBJ/BREP/IGES), a `shapes` convenience, and STEP-only AP242 `dimensions` / `geomTolerances` / `datums`.

Export OCCT shapes with `ExportManager.export(shapes:format:to:deflection:)` (async throws). `ExportFormat` is `.obj`/`.ply`/`.step`/`.brep`/`.gltf`/`.glb`; meshed formats use `deflection` (default 0.1), STEP/BREP are exact. Multiple shapes write one indexed file each (out.0.ext, out.1.ext).

Mesh I/O is synchronous and OCCT-free: `MeshIO.load(contentsOf:weldEpsilon:)` picks the reader by extension and welds vertices (default 1e-4); `MeshIO.write(_:to:format:asciiSTL:)` picks the writer by `format` or extension. PMX and `.x` are read-only (`MeshFormat.canWrite` is false); writing them throws `MeshError.unsupported`.

`Mesh` is a pure value type (`positions: [SIMD3<Float>]`, `indices: [UInt32]`) with `vertexCount`/`triangleCount`/`bounds`; build from soups via `Mesh.welded(_:epsilon:)` or `Mesh.indexedSoup(_:)`. Native readers `STL` / `OBJ` / `PLY` are callable directly on `Data`.

`ScriptManifest` (+ `BodyDescriptor`, `ManifestMetadata`) is plain Codable (no OCCT to decode); ISO-8601 dates; `BodyDescriptor.color` decodes a `[r,g,b,a]` JSON array to `SIMD4<Float>?`. Use `ImportProgressClosure { fraction, step in ... }` (with an optional `cancelCheck`) for STEP/IGES progress, and `TopologyGraph.exportForML()` / `exportJSON()` for ML graph export.
